guidance: Use non-interactive print mode for bounded automation.
Use --max-budget-usd for dollar budget limits in print mode.
Use --max-turns when the installed Claude Code CLI supports it.
Use --no-session-persistence for bounded automation sessions.
Token cost scales with context size.
Keep context small, clear between unrelated tasks, and use compaction deliberately.
Use usage/status signals during interactive operation.
Use filesystem sandbox and permission rules to constrain file and tool access.
Control reads, edits, and network domains explicitly.
Use MCP tool search or equivalent deferral so tool definitions do not consume unnecessary context.
Keep MCP server instructions concise and put critical details first.
Input, output, tool, and thinking tokens count toward context and billing boundaries.
Preserve required thinking/tool-use blocks when using API-level tool results.
Cache stable prompt prefixes when repetitive context is unavoidable.
Keep volatile task state after stable context so caches remain useful.
Never commit API keys.
Use GitHub Secrets for provider keys.
Limit workflow permissions and require review before merge.
Use explicit task context and durable AGENTS.md guidance.
Keep AGENTS.md practical and concise.
Codex tracks context-window pressure and can compact long tasks.
Structured logs can include token-count telemetry for completed responses.

must_not_use: raw ChatGPT transcript as executor prompt
codex_context_governance.compaction_policy: Summaries may preserve task goal, scope, policy boundaries, validations, changed files, and blockers only.
codex_context_governance.audit_policy: Codex audit receives evidence packets, final reports, changed-file summaries, validation results, and policy status rather than raw secrets or unbounded outputs.

claude_code_runtime_governance.max_turns_policy: Use --max-turns for live launches only when the installed Claude Code CLI exposes the flag; otherwise record the unsupported capability and rely on max_budget_usd plus TimeoutSeconds.

mcp_tool_context_governance.tool_loading_policy: Prefer deferred tool discovery/search over loading every tool schema into the executor context.
mcp_tool_context_governance.instruction_policy: Keep server instructions short, task-specific, and front-loaded with critical boundaries.
mcp_tool_context_governance.specbridge_policy: MCP resources exposed by SpecBridge remain read-only until a dedicated execution contract authorizes mutation.

multi_agent_slice_governance.slice_prompt_policy: Each Claude Code slice receives only its contract, scope, read-only context, exclusive write paths, validations, stop conditions, and final report expectations.
multi_agent_slice_governance.parallelism_policy: Parallel executors require non-overlapping exclusive_write scopes and a preflight budget check before launch.
multi_agent_slice_governance.budget_policy: Budget is tracked per launch and aggregated by coordinator evidence; no slice may raise budget or widen tools after live execution starts unless the contract explicitly allows it.
multi_agent_slice_governance.usage_limit_policy: Usage-limit or budget-limit failures are runtime evidence, not proof of implementation failure. Retry only when the contract allows a bounded retry.

evidence_requirements: runtime execution artifact for live launches
runtime result or summary when applicable
